workflow validation metadata and default tool behavior

workflow validation metadata and default tool behavior
workflow validation metadata and default tool behavior

acceptance_criteria.statement: The target architecture no longer depends on a wrapper CLI or wrapper-owned artifact store.
The design assigns native workflow metadata responsibilities to ticket-api, spec-api, doc-api, future test-api, and future log-api.
Existing CLI, MCP, and HTTP surfaces are the default thin interfaces over shared workflow behavior.

evidence_requirements.description: Architecture direction and acceptance criteria document native store ownership, explicit future store roles, and thin transport behavior.
Spec scan and spec health validation succeed for the migrated expectation-oriented contract.

expected_properties.statement: Workflow validation metadata is owned by native shared-library stores instead of wrapper-only artifacts.
CLI, MCP, and HTTP surfaces remain thin interfaces over shared workflow behavior.
doc-api, test-api, and log-api responsibilities remain explicit in the target workflow architecture.

fulfillment_summaries.detail: The Architecture direction and Acceptance criteria sections define native store ownership, future store roles, and thin transport behavior.
Focused spec health and scan validation completed after the pilot migration.
